title: Liberty Lantern
short_description: A situation lights up the lantern — now pick which freedom protects it: speech, press, religion, assembly, a fair trial and more.
description: Hold up the Liberty Lantern and a real-life situation glows into view — 'a newspaper prints a story the mayor dislikes', 'people march peacefully for a cause', 'someone accused of a crime gets a fair trial' — and you choose which freedom or right is keeping it safe. It teaches civics through scenarios instead of definitions: the core freedoms of speech, press, religion, assembly and petition, plus fair treatment, voting and equal protection, and the idea that rights apply to everyone. Grade bands ramp from simple everyday rights, to naming the specific freedoms, to trickier scenarios about fair trials, equality and privacy. Correct calls and streaks earn bonus points. A free online rights, freedoms and citizenship civics game for kids in grades 1–5, feeding your score and GP on OnlyGrins.
controls: Mouse / tap the freedom or right that protects the situation.
